content: The Earth moves around the Sun in a big circle called an orbit. The Earth is also tilted a little bit, like it’s leaning over while it goes around the Sun. Because the Earth is tilted, different parts of Earth get more sunlight at different times of the year. When your part of the Earth gets more sunlight, it feels warmer and we call that summer. When your part of the Earth gets less sunlight, it feels colder and we call that winter. Between summer and winter, there are two times when the sunlight is almost the same, and we call those spring and fall. It takes one whole year for the Earth to go all the way around the Sun and change through all the seasons. So, the seasons happen because the Earth’s tilt changes how much sunlight different places get as it moves around the Sun.
